title: Delicate Earthy Middle Eastern Eggs Shakshuka
description: A light and earthy Middle Eastern dish featuring dried red chillies and flaky pastry, perfect for a wholesome breakfast. This ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly recipe is made with wholesome ingredients and gentle cooking methods.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chopped bell peppers and cook until they are tender.
Add the crushed dried red chillies and cook for one minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook until they are tender and the mixture is heated through.
Make four wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake at 375 degrees for 15-20 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the top.
Serve the shakshuka hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
